title: mimulus guttatus subsp. scouleri
intro: Mimulus guttatus subsp. scouleri, also known as Skunkflower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Phrymaceae family and is native to the Pacific coast of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is also known to have anxiolytic and antidepressant properties. The health benefits of Skunkflower include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for mood disorders and skin issues. Skunkflower can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is often used in herbal preparations to promote calmness and well-being.
